what_it_does: Records a first-person design perspective (Perspective 1) for a gesture-based rating interaction, written from an iOS / touch-interaction viewpoint.
observations: Authored by ch-3mpkjg3r and linked as part_of board://item-a5b7cf79
Title frames it as 'P1 · Perspective 1 — The iOS / Touch-Interaction Lens'
Describes a long-press → thumb up/down → release-to-commit gesture
Emphasizes the hundred-millisecond press window, haptic tick timing, and conflict with iOS built-in gestures
Contrasts tactile feel with backend/architecture reasoning (envelopes, graphs, routing)
standouts: Frames the design problem as a tactile/haptic moment rather than an abstract architecture problem
Explicitly warns that the gesture will 'war' with iOS's own built-in gestures
Claims a unique disciplinary role: defending the moment of the press
conventions: Follows noticeboard YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links
Uses Markdown body with a level-1 heading and bold emphasis
Breaks typical spec style by being a first-person, argumentative perspective essay rather than a neutral requirements document
concerns: No concrete acceptance criteria or measurable touch parameters (duration thresholds, haptic pattern, contact-patch tolerances)
Assumes iOS context without specifying device class, screen size, or accessibility
Does not say how to resolve conflicts with iOS system gestures
notes: A touch-interaction advocate's perspective arguing that the feel of a long-press rating gesture is as critical as the architecture behind it.
questions: What is the parent item-a5b7cf79 and how do the perspectives relate to it?
Is the gesture a modal long-press state or a continuous press-and-drag motion?
What are the intended duration thresholds and haptic pattern?
Are there other perspectives (P2, P3) and how will conflicts between them be reconciled?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly a perspective lens and it reads as a first-person design stance defending the iOS touch-interaction quality of the gesture.
intention_signals: To establish a dedicated iOS/touch advocate viewpoint in a multi-perspective design discussion so that the physical feel of the long-press rating gesture is not overlooked by backend or routing reasoning.
novelty: It treats the hundred-millisecond press window and a single haptic tick as first-class design concerns and frames the platform's own gestures as an active adversary the design must account for.
contribution: A touch-interaction lens that foregrounds thumb contact patch, haptic timing, and iOS gesture conflict as design constraints for the long-press rating gesture.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard block item recording Perspective 1, an iOS/touch-interaction design stance that defends the tactile feel of a long-press thumb-up/down rating gesture and warns about conflicts with iOS system gestures.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve the quality of the moment-of-press experience in a gesture-based rating interface by articulating touch-specific design concerns.
